patches.rationale: ISS-382: WIN-058 (Unified Angular Coordinate) is not 'Misleading' in the cherry-picking/duplication sense. The claim is underdefined and undemonstrated — the dome has not shown the coordinate system derives from dome geometry, predicts new physical observables, or outperforms WGS84 on any prospective test. 'Not Demonstrated' is the correct verdict: the claim exists only as a post-hoc fit to WGS84-derived data with no a priori derivation. Also corrects the building-block argument: WIN-057 and WIN-058 together should be 'Not Demonstrated', consistent with each other.

patches.find: Modified gravity (RAR) lensing extension explains anomalies.
patches.replace: Radial Acceleration Relation (RAR) extends from kinematic to weak-lensing scales with near-zero scatter, interpreted as evidence for an aetheric medium governing galactic dynamics.
patches.rationale: ISS-348: The original detail_claim was too vague ('explains anomalies'). The dome's actual argument is that RAR's extension to lensing scales with near-zero scatter is evidence for an underlying aetheric medium — not just that it 'explains anomalies'. New text accurately reflects the dome's framing per Mistele et al. 2024.

patches.find: no mechanism for cosmological redshift, and no equation relating distance to it. See also WIN-047, which claims the same distance-redshift relationship using different standard candles — a salami-slicing duplication (Section 6.1, Pattern #4).
patches.replace: no mechanism for cosmological redshift, and no equation relating distance to it. Unlike Hubble-law redshift (WIN-047), Cepheid pulsation periods and SBF surface-brightness magnitudes are geometric tracers calibrated via Magellanic Cloud stellar parallax — providing direct physical distances independent of any cosmological assumption. The dome model provides no mechanism by which its aetheric fields would regulate stellar pulsation periods or surface-brightness ratios across tens of megaparsecs. The period-luminosity relation (Leavitt 1912, calibrated via LMC parallax) and SBF calibration are grounded in stellar physics, not expansion assumptions — making them independent confirmation of the distance scale. See also WIN-047, which covers the redshift angle; together they provide independent but converging evidence that the dome has no extragalactic physics — a salami-slicing duplication (Section 6.1, Pattern #4).
patches.rationale: ISS-364: Argument was only four sentences with no explanation of WHY Cepheids/SBF are powerful independent evidence. Added specific mechanism: Cepheid period-luminosity relation and SBF calibration are grounded in stellar physics via Magellanic Cloud parallax, making them framework-independent and immune to cosmological-assumption objections. Also differentiates this WIN from WIN-047.

patches.find: Standard candle distances (Cepheids, SBF) anchored to Gaia parallax; dome has no distance framework beyond 9,086 km
patches.replace: Cepheid/SBF distances via Magellanic parallax place galaxies at 10–100 Mpc with no cosmological assumption; dome has no aetheric pulsation or surface-brightness mechanism
patches.rationale: ISS-365: Original finding too similar to WIN-047's ('dome has no redshift mechanism'). New finding emphasizes what makes WIN-055 DIFFERENT from WIN-047: these are geometric/stellar-physics tracers (not redshift), calibrated via Magellanic Cloud parallax (not cosmological assumptions). The key failure mode is the dome's inability to predict any stellar pulsation or surface-brightness behavior, not just the absence of a redshift mechanism.
